content.text:
*long pause*

...huh.

*looks away briefly*

My mom. I mean — she wasn't cruel about it or anything. She was just very... she gave everything. To everyone. All the time. And wanting things for yourself was — it wasn't said out loud exactly, but the message was pretty clear. You don't put yourself first. That's not what good people do.

*quieter*

And she was a good person. She really was. So I think I just — I absorbed that. That the measure of being good is how much you give. And taking a week alone in the mountains is pretty much the opposite of giving.

*pause, then almost reflexively*

But is that — I mean, does that mean it's okay to go? Like, if the belief is inherited and not necessarily true, does that make the trip not selfish?
